question.text: Which of the following best describes obsessive love?

options.text: A form of love characterized by obsessive attempts to possess or control someone.
A deep, mutual, and healthy romantic relationship.
A casual romantic interest that fades quickly over time.
A type of love where partners share equal power and control.

prediction.answer_text: A form of love characterized by obsessive attempts to possess or control someone.

metadata.retrieval_query: Which of the following best describes obsessive love?
metadata.evidence_preview:
[1] Obsessive love - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Obsessive_love
Obsessive love Obsessive love is characterized by obsessive or compulsive attempts to possess or control an individual, especially triggered (or even intensified) by rejection.[2] Obsessive love can also be distinguished from other forms of romantic love by its one-sidedness and repulsed approaches.[3] Rejection is the "ultimate nightmare" to an obsessive lover, who can not let go when confronted with disinterest or the loss of a partner.[4]

[2] Recognizing Obsessive Love: A Comprehensive Guide
URL: https://reachlink.com/advice/obsessive-compulsive-disorder/obsessive-love-when-intense-feelings-become-dangerous/
. In this Article What is obsessive love? Definition and core characteristics Obsessive love describes a pattern of intense, consuming preoccupation with another person that overrides rational thought and disrupts healthy functioning. It’s the kind of fixation where thoughts about someone take over your day, where you can’t focus on work, sleep, or even basic self-care because your mind keeps circling back to them
